metadata.title: Auto political influence + labor — UAW, the dealer-franchise lobby, IRA EV credits, CAFE rules, and the 2009 bailout legacy (both sides, dated)
metadata.status: the UAW strike + votes, the dealer-franchise laws, the IRA EV credits + 2025 termination, the CAFE rollback, and the 2009 bailout are FACT (NLRB/union, Congress, EPA, Treasury records); the policy debates are presented BOTH ways; OPPOSED intra-industry interests (UAW vs makers; dealers vs Tesla) are kept distinct (composition/division guard)
metadata.frame: Sub-block of the auto epic (#177) — the political/labor layer, where 'the auto industry' is NOT one actor: labor (UAW) opposes the makers; the dealer lobby (NADA) opposes direct-sellers (Tesla); the maker lobby (AAI) fights R2R (#178). Threads: the UAW's 2023 stand-up strike + 2024 Southern organizing (a win and a loss); state dealer-FRANCHISE laws blocking direct sales; the IRA EV credits + their 2025 termination; the CAFE/EPA tailpipe rollback; and the 2009 bailout legacy. Attaches to US_Auto_Makers, Tesla, US_Government, Alliance_Auto_Innovation.

the_uaw: Labor vs the makers: the UAW (Shawn Fain) ran a 2023 'STAND-UP STRIKE' — the first simultaneous strike against all Big Three (Sept-Oct 2023) — winning record contracts (~25% wage gains over the term + COLA restoration). It then turned to organizing foreign-owned Southern plants: a WIN at VW Chattanooga (Apr 2024, the first foreign-owned US auto plant to unionize) but a LOSS at Mercedes' Alabama plant (May 2024). Fain has floated aligning contracts for a potential 2028 broad action. grade: fact.
the_dealer_lobby: Dealers vs direct-sellers: state DEALER-FRANCHISE LAWS (defended by NADA, the dealers' lobby) bar manufacturers from selling directly to consumers in many states — the structural barrier that has blocked or limited Tesla, Rivian, and Lucid's direct-sales model state by state. This is an intra-'industry' conflict: incumbent dealers vs the EV-natives. grade: fact.
the_subsidies: Subsidy + rule swings, dated: the Inflation Reduction Act (2022) created the $7,500 consumer EV credit + 45X manufacturing credits + domestic-content + foreign-entity-of-concern (FEOC) rules designed to exclude Chinese content — then the 2025 budget law TERMINATED the $7,500 consumer EV credit (effective ~30 Sep 2025), and the EPA/NHTSA moved to ROLL BACK the Biden-era CAFE/tailpipe standards that had favored EVs. Both ways: (A) industrial-policy/climate rationale vs (B) market-distortion/cost critique. grade: fact (the credits + their termination + the rollback).
the_bailout_legacy: The precedent: the 2008-09 financial crisis drove GM and Chrysler into managed Chapter 11 + a ~$80B federal TARP auto rescue ('Government Motors'), restructuring the Big Three and the UAW (the two-tier wage that the 2023 strike partly reversed). It established the template that the Detroit makers are systemically important enough to rescue. grade: fact.
the_two_sides: Both ways, dated, with the composition guard: (A) one reading treats 'the auto industry' as a bloc lobbying for subsidies + protection; (B) the accurate reading is that the industry is INTERNALLY DIVIDED — UAW vs makers on wages, dealers (NADA) vs makers/Tesla on direct sales, the maker lobby (AAI) vs independents on repair (#178), and EV-natives vs incumbents on subsidies/standards. So 'auto-industry influence' is a set of CONFLICTING lobbies, not a single mind. grade: both framings noted; the divided-interests reading is the warranted one.
the_honest_reading: The auto sector's politics are a web of OPPOSED interests, not one lobby: the UAW (2023 strike win; 2024 VW-Chattanooga win + Mercedes-Alabama loss) pushes against the makers; the dealer lobby (NADA + franchise laws) blocks the EV-natives' direct sales; the maker lobby (AAI) fights right-to-repair; and policy swung hard — the IRA EV credits (2022) terminated in 2025 with a CAFE/tailpipe rollback — atop the 2009 bailout precedent. The dated facts are clear; the key analytical point (per the composition guard) is that 'the auto industry' does not act with one mind. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

sources.name: AP — UAW reaches record deals ending the 2023 stand-up strike
Reuters — VW Chattanooga workers vote to join UAW (Apr 2024); Mercedes Alabama rejects (May 2024)
Reuters — US $7,500 EV tax credit ends Sept 30, 2025 (2025 budget law)
EPA/Reuters — rollback of Biden-era vehicle tailpipe/CAFE standards (2025)
Treasury — TARP Automotive Industry Financing Program (the 2009 bailout)
